sources: Web search: קריית אתגרים רעננה תעשייה

facilities.name_he: תחנת טורבינות גז רעננה — חברת החשמל לישראל
facilities.name_en: Raanana Gas Turbine Station — Israel Electric Corporation (IEC)
evidence: Borehole 'נד תחנת טורבינות גז רעננה' named directly for this facility
PFHxS 1.160 µg/L (1,160% of standard) + PFOA 0.524 µg/L (524%) detected July 2025
PFAS profile dominated by PFHxS and PFOA — consistent with AFFF fire-fighting foam (3M FC-203, Ansul AFFF)
Gas turbines require AFFF for fire suppression (NFPA 750 standard)
facilities.notes: First PFAS detection July 2025 — single measurement, confirmatory sampling required Q3 2026. Regulatory reporting to Ministry of Environmental Protection and Water Authority required immediately.

evidence: Borehole 'נד פז הנופר' named directly for this facility
Benzene peak 10 µg/L (2019-10-23) = 200% of drinking water standard (5 µg/L)
Toluene peak ~30 µg/L (exact date unclear)
Benzene detections spanning 2011–2024 consistent with ongoing UST leakage
Intermittent pattern (spikes and near-zero periods) characteristic of UST source
Fuel station UST infrastructure documented at this coordinates
facilities.notes: UST inspection not documented. Post-2023 benzene values ~0.6 µg/L suggest continued low-level leakage. PFAS not yet measured at this borehole.

facilities.name_he: קריית אתגרים — מפעלי תעשייה כימית ופרמצבטיקה
facilities.name_en: Kiryat Atgarim — Chemical and Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Cluster
facilities.type: Industrial zone — mixed chemical, pharmaceutical, electronics manufacturing
evidence: נת רעננה 1: TCE peak 817 µg/L (2019-07-22) = 10,893% of 7.5 µg/L standard
נת רעננה 2: TCE up to 24 µg/L (2015), PCE 5.7 µg/L (2025)
נת רעננה 3: PCE peak 105.5 µg/L (2024-09-04) = 1,055% of 10 µg/L standard; classic PCE→TCE decay chain
CVOC contamination pattern consistent with industrial degreasing and solvent use
2021 Report (page 15-16): zone ranked #2/18 among all coastal industrial zones, severity index 7/8 (very_high)
Groundwater flow direction E→W; all CVOC boreholes downgradient of industrial cluster
פ רעננה 25 (private production well, 600m SW): TCE 8.9–14.1 µg/L (2023-2025), plume reaching production well
facilities.notes: Attribution to specific companies within the industrial zone is not possible without facility-level sampling. The contamination profile (TCE dominant, PCE secondary with decay chain to cis-DCE) is consistent with chlorinated solvent use in degreasing operations — typical of electronics, metals, and pharmaceutical manufacturing. Expert investigation required to identify specific emission source(s). Source: 2021 Report methodology, Excel borehole measurements.

evidence: Single measurement 2011: all parameters below detection limit
No industrial contamination detected
Located ~300m west of nt_1 — potentially upgradient or in low-concentration zone
facilities.notes: No attributable industrial source — borehole used as background reference point. Updated sampling required after 15-year gap. No facility attribution applicable.

contamination_pathway.plume_interpretation: TCE plume from קריית אתגרים migrating westward; reached private production well פ רעננה 25 by 2023. Most intense contamination at nt_1 (closest to source, highest concentrations). nt_2 and nt_3 show secondary plume at lower/intermediate concentrations.

data_gaps: No TAHAL 2008 data for Raanana — monitoring started 2011
Specific facilities within קריית אתגרים not individually investigated
PFAS not measured at most boreholes (only nd_turbine, July 2025)
Toluene peak date at nd_paz unclear
UST inspection at Paz station not documented
AFFF inventory and incident history at IEC turbine station not obtained
